IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
This work is taking place in the Bessels
Green Road area. To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our
overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close
and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage
and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work
in your area.
Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely trim trees away from the
overhead cables. This work will cover approximately 200 metres across 3 sections of the network.
Due to the scale of the work, A longer shutdown is required due to volume of work needing to be
carried out. Completing this work in one visit will help reduce the risk of future unplanned outages
and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular inspections of our equipment in your area. This has identified 5 poles that need upgrading. Whilst the power is off our engineers will be installing new electricity cables between the poles. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future. To complete the work safely, we need to turn your power off to upgrade the equipment. To ensure the power is off for the shortest possible time, our engineers will be carrying out preparation works on the 22.06.2026. This work is taking place in the Devils Hill Wood area.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: Our engineers removing overhead electricity cables and replacing them with new underground electricity cables and removing a redundant pole mounted transformer. Doing this will give the third party their desired outcome and reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future whilst the works are completed safely. To connect the new cables and complete the work safely we need to turn your power off. To ensure the power is off for the shortest possible time, our engineers may carry out preparation works prior to the power cut. This work is taking place in the Broadway area.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: This is the planned power cut that was previously scheduled for the 7th of May 2026. We are removing sections of the high voltage network and installing new underground electricity cables. We will also be disconnecting a generator which is currently connected to part of the network to restore these customers back to the main network. To complete the work safely we need to turn your power off. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate some customers may have experienced unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. To ensure the power is off for the shortest possible time, our contractors Avidety may carry out preparation works prior to the planned power cut. This work is taking place near Prinkle Lane.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
This work is taking place near Church Wood
Drive.Reason for the planned power cut
What are we doing?
Where are we working?
This work is taking place near Church Wood
Drive. Please turn over for useful information.
We regularly inspect our electrical equipment to ensure it is safe and efficient. We need to examine
transformers on our electricity poles to check they are functioning properly. This essential work
helps to provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, reducing the risk of any unplanned
power cuts in the future.To keep everyone safe while we carry out these works, we will need to temporarily turn off your
electricity. We are taking extra measures to limit the time your power is off and we'll do our best to
cause as little disruption as possible. Doing this will improve the reliability of the electricity network
in the future.

Steps.Message: Hi, this is a message from UK Power Networks regarding the planned power cut that is currently in progress today. Good news our customer support vehicle has now arrived in your area. The agent will be knocking on the doors of the known vulnerable customers needing support in the area first, before parking up to offer support, so if you would like them to come and see you, please call us free on 105 to make us aware. Once they have done this, they will remain at their vehicle where they can provide free hot drinks, charging points, Wi-Fi and face-to-face support. Logs.Comment: Hi, this is a message from UK Power Networks regarding the planned power cut that is currently in progress today. Good news our customer support vehicle has now arrived in your area. The agent will be knocking on the doors of the known vulnerable customers needing support in the area first, before parking up to offer support, so if you would like them to come and see you, please call us free on 105 to make us aware. Once they have done this, they will remain at their vehicle where they can provide free hot drinks, charging points, Wi-Fi and face-to-face support. If you have any further questions or concerns, do not hesitate to call us free on 105.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely cut trees away from the overhead cables. By carefully planning this work in advance, we have been able to minimise the duration of the power interruption. This essential work will cover approximately 1 section of the overhead network. Doing this helps reduce the risk of future unplanned power cuts and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable. This work is taking place in the Green Man Lane area.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: A third party has arranged for trees near the overhead electricity lines to be trimmed and has asked us to temporarily switch off the power so this can be carried out safely. Some of these trees have grown close to the cables and could cause damage, particularly during poor weather. Allowing this work to take place helps reduce the risk of future unplanned power cuts and keeps the network safe and reliable. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so a tree surgeon can safely cut trees away from the overhead cables. This work will help reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts in the future as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, a longer shutdown is required due to volume of work needing to be carried out. This work is taking place near The Tye.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: Following a customers request, we are installing a new Independent Distribution Network Operator (IDNO) substation to the electricity network. This equipment controls and delivers the electricity into your area. You may see our engineers completing preparation works prior to the planned power cut to allow for a short interruption on the day. Once completed this will give the third party their desired outcome as we have a duty to provide electricity when requested. This work will also ensure the electricity network can uphold the amount of electricity being used in your area. This work is taking place in the Lucks Hill area in West Malling.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 00:56 - Thanks for your patience today, we're happy to say our staff have now restored power to all but one property in the Balas Drive area. I'm sorry for any disruption we caused by having to turn off your power to repair unexpected damage to our cabling by another company.

We'd still like to keep you informed if your electricity hasn't been restored. Please call us free any time on 08003163105 if we haven't already reached out to you by phone or in person at your property.

We apologise for the outage and understand that being without power can be challenging. Rest assured that our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep your lights on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Talie at 00:27 - We're very sorry we've had to switch off your electricity. Our engineers have turned off the power in the Balas Drive area for safety so they can repair damage to our cabling by a third party. Our estimated time to have your power back on is between 02:00 and 03:00, however, this may need to change later depending on how our works progress. If you'd like more information about why we sometimes have to switch power off to work; here's a short video https://UKPN.short.gy/Isol. We'll keep you updated with progress.

Logs.Comment:
Sent by Talie at 03:41 - We can now confirm that all power in the Green Wrythe Lane area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected fault with some of our network equipment, which our engineers have now replaced.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Our engineers have arrived on site in the Green Wrythe Lane area, the estimated time of 06:00-07:00 hasn't changed.

We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can.

We'll update you with all of our progress, but if you need to speak to us, call our team on 08003163105 any time. If you would like more information about what we think the fault is; here's a short YouTube video https://UKPN.short.gy/fuse Sent by Talie at 03:09.

Sent by Talie at 02:36 - Engineers are on their way. At this point we think the issue is a fault with equipment in your local substation in the Green Wrythe Lane area. We didn't know this was going to happen, so time frames we provide throughout are estimated and are subject to change.

Our estimated time to have power back on is currently between 06:00 and 07:00, but after we arrive and assess the situation this may change. The reason for this is there can sometimes be a more complex underlying issue found by our engineer's tests which may take longer, and if not we may get power back on sooner.

All of our texts are written and sent personally by our team, and we'll let you know once our staff arrive.

Sent by Talie at 04:28 - Our engineers have now restored power to all 14 customers in the Princes Riseborough, HP27 0 area, by diverting power around the overhead cable fault. We know a power cut can be difficult; we're sorry for any disruption this unplanned power cut caused.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Kevin at 07:06 - We can now confirm that all power in the Pinner Road area should be back on.

Our staff are trying to find the root cause and fix it permanently, however with this kind of issue it's likely that power will go off multiple times before we're able to find and fix it. For now we have restored your power as quickly as we can.

If your power isn't on or you would like to talk to us, call us for free on 08003163105. We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
We're sorry your power has gone off again.

We believe there may be an underlying issue causing the recent power cuts, but due to the nature of the electricity network, the heat created by a developing fault can cause a break in a cable to reseal itself. This can make it difficult to locate, so while our engineers are testing our equipment thoroughly to pinpoint the issue, this kind of problem may interrupt power multiple times before it's possible for us to fix.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

I'll keep you updated. Sent by Kevin at 06:31

Steps.Message:
Sent by Kevin at 09:39 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Roland at 08:39 - Engineers are on their way. At this point we think the issue is a fault with equipment in your local substation in the High Street area. We didn't know this was going to happen, so time frames we provide throughout are estimated and are subject to change.

Our estimated time to have power back on is currently between 11:00 and 12:00, but after we arrive and assess the situation this may change. The reason for this is there can sometimes be a more complex underlying issue found by our engineer's tests which may take longer, and if not we may get power back on sooner.

All of our texts are written and sent personally by our team, and we'll let you know once our staff arrive.

Sent by Kevin at 08:50 - We can now confirm that all power in the Remington Road area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected fault with some of our network equipment, which our engineers have now replaced.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Remington Road area, the estimated time of 08:00 and 09:00 hasn't changed. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can. We'll update you with all of our progress, but if you need to speak to us, call our team on 08003163105 any time. If you would like more information about what we think the fault is; here's a short YouTube video https://UKPN.short.gy/fuse Sent by Roland at 08:08.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Kevin at 07:07 - We're sorry for the disruption to your power today, especially for those who may be more vulnerable without the electric. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. For more information just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR If you have any further concerns or questions though, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Great news - Our engineers have now restored power to all 364 customers in the London, E4 7 area by diverting power around the faulty underground cables.

Thank you for your patience while our team worked hard to resolve the issue safely. We apologise the outage lasted longer than anticipated and we appreciate your understanding.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
We know the time frame we initially estimated is now approaching. Our engineers are continuing their work to repair our underground network in the London, E4 7 area.

We're sorry you've been impacted by this, and we'll provide you an update as soon as possible. Sent by Roland at 08:06.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Kevin at 07:51 - We can now confirm that all power in the Duckett Road area should be back on.

Our staff are trying to find the root cause and fix it permanently, however with this kind of issue it's likely that power will go off multiple times before we're able to find and fix it. For now we have restored your power as quickly as we can.

If your power isn't on or you would like to talk to us, call us for free on 08003163105. We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our staff have now arrived on site in the Duckett Road area. They will start running tests at our substation to see how the local network is operating.

It's quite common for power to be restored briefly and go off again; this is our engineers using testing equipment to energise different sections of our network - this helps to narrow down any possible issues.

Our cabling network is often broken down by smaller sites, and our staff may also check these to test for any problems, and they may also walk the cabling route to see if there are any signs of malfunction that have appeared on the surface. Our time frame hasn't been adjusted, so for now I'll let you know more news as soon as our staff update us.

Sent by Kevin at 07:05.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Kevin at 06:25 - Our staff are on the way to the Duckett Road area, and our estimated time frame to have power back on is currently between 08:30 and 09:30. However, this could change once our staff have assessed the local network.

We believe the power cuts you have been experiencing are caused by a fault with one of the cables. Developing faults can start very small, and so can be hard to detect but each power cut usually makes it easier to see, which means our specialist staff can detect it, and work on the repair to prevent further outages.

I'll let you know once our teams arrive.

Steps.Message:
Great news - Our engineers have now restored power to all 679customers in the TN12 and ME18 area by diverting power around the overhead fault.

Thank you for your patience while our team worked hard to resolve this issue safely; we're pleased that our engineers were able restore the power within the timeframe we had estimated.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Sent by Luke at 06:46
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Talie at 05:38 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is an overhead cable fault that initially interrupted power to 679 properties. However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 475 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment. We're currently estimating power will be on between 07:30-08:30, but this may change depending on what our staff find. Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage affects multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you expect. We're arranging staff to attend ASAP, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting electricity. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to get as many customers back on as we can.

Once available, our engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, they will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will work to locate the issue and make repairs, which may take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing but we'll always give you the clearest information we have.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Sent by Luke at 13:28 - Our engineers have completed their investigations.

There's going to be a lot of work required to repair the issue safely, so we have decided to use temporary generators to provide power. To allow time for us to get the generators out to your area and connect them, we're now estimating we'll have power back on between 17:00 and 18:00.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 08:31 - Some customers have told us that they want to be notified about a power cut before it happens. We're sorry we couldn't give you advance notice about this power cut. The issue happened suddenly and without warning, leaving us with no time to alert customers.
Our electricity network is regularly inspected and maintained to help prevent outages, but occasionally an unexpected fault can lead to an unplanned power cut. It's similar to a car that's been fully serviced and passed its MOT but still breaks down without warning.
Thank you for your understanding and we will keep you updated about todays outage until you are restored.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

We'll update you with all of our progress, but if you need to speak to us, call our team on 08003163105 any time. Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 14:35 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Luke at 13:54 - Thanks for bearing with us over today. Our team of cable jointers have now identified the suspected section of faulted underground cabling using their specialist equipment.

Our digging team will now begin excavations, and this can take some time depending on the surface material, and how much space is needed to allow our engineers to assess the fault and make their repairs safely. 

Our estimated time frame of 22:00 - 23:00 doesn't need to change currently. We'll continue to keep you updated, but our team are always here if you have any questions.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Kevin at 06:53 - Our staff are on the way to the Woodmere Avenue area, and our estimated time frame to have power back on is currently between 09:30 and 10:30. However, this could change once our staff have assessed the local network.

We believe the power cuts you have been experiencing are caused by a fault with one of the cables. I'll let you know once our teams arrive.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: We were investigating a high voltage overhead electricity line fault which caused a power cut in the local area.

Logs.Comment:
Sent by Luke at 11:51 -

We're sorry for the disruption to your power today, especially for those who may be more vulnerable without the electric. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. For more information just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR
 
If you have any further concerns or questions though, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress.

Sent by Luke at 08:21 - We believe our engineers have been able to restore power to all 421 customers in the Canterbury area by diverting the power around the overhead fault. We're pleased that we have been able to restore power sooner than we estimated.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

We know that being without power can be worrying, but please rest assured that our staff work around the clock to keep the lights on. If you have any concerns or questions, give us a call any time.

Our engineers have arrived on site in the Canterbury area and are investigating the fault.

You may find your power is going on and off; that is normal for this type of issue and is due to our engineers working to divert power remotely. By doing this, we've managed to restore power to another 47 customers. There are now 47 properties still affected and using an average time frame we're estimating all power will be on between 09:30 and 10:30, but this may change later depending on what our staff find.

We'll keep sending SMS updates until everybody is restored. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Sent by Luke at 07:26.

Steps.Message: Hi, Bev from the messaging team here; just a quick recap on what's happened during the power cut so far. Our engineers are still working to repair the underground cable fault in the London Steet area. A full road closure is needed to enable them to safely complete the fix. We are still aiming to have the power back on between 16:30 and 17:30. If your power is back on now, please follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to stop SMS updates. I'm sorry for the continued disruption, but I'll keep you informed with news from site. If you have any concerns, please call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Kevin at 13:06 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 11:13 - We can now confirm that all power in the Hillside Road area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected fault with some of our network equipment, which our engineers have now replaced.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 10:38 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 10:22 - Some customers have told us that they want to be notified about a power cut before it happens. We're sorry we couldn't give you advance notice about this power cut. The issue happened suddenly and without warning, leaving us with no time to alert customers.
Our electricity network is regularly inspected and maintained to help prevent outages, but occasionally an unexpected fault can lead to an unplanned power cut. It's similar to a car that's been fully serviced and passed its MOT but still breaks down without warning.
Thank you for your understanding and we will keep you updated about todays outage until you are restored.

This means its going to take us past our current time so we are now working towards between 18:30 and 19:30 to have your power back on.

We know how disruptive being without power can be, we are working as fast as we can. If you're worried about anyone who may be more vulnerable in your property who is affected by todays outage, please don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Bev at 13:01 - Thanks for bearing with us over today. Our team of cable jointers have now identified the fault which is was found to be on our underground cabling network.

Our digging team will now begin excavations, and this can take some time depending on the surface material, and how much space is needed to allow our engineers to assess the fault and make their repairs safely. 

Our estimated time frame of 14:30-15:30 doesn't need to change currently. We'll continue to keep you updated, but our team are always here if you have any questions.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Roland at 09:17 - We can now confirm that all power in the Woodhouse Road area should now be back on.

Upon investigation, the issue was found to be a problem with some of our network equipment, which our engineers have now repaired. Although there were power cuts some months ago, now that our engineers have rectified this, we're pleased to say we do not believe it will cause any subsequent issues with your power.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the recent outages. If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our staff have now arrived on site in the Woodhouse Road area. Due to there being multiple power cuts over a short space of time, our engineers are going to be testing if the network is still healthy.

They will start at the substation, and often apply specialist equipment that may turn your power back on for a brief period, so don't worry if your power comes on and goes off again. They will also check smaller sites which run along our cabling route, these break our network down into smaller sections and can help pinpoint any possible problems.

Our estimated time frame of 11:00 and 12:00 hasn't changed, so I'll let you know more once our staff update us. Sent by Kevin at 08:52.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
I appreciate your continued patience this morning. I know having power cuts as often as this must be very disruptive, and our staff are doing everything they can to get this resolved.

They are still testing our network in the area, and depending on the local infrastructure this can take some time. However, once they update us we'll know if our estimated time frame needs to change and why, and I'll let you know as soon as we hear.

In the meantime, if we can offer any support or advice, our team are always available to talk on 105.

Sent by Kevin at 10:54.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our staff have now arrived on site in the Mount Avenue area. They will start running tests at our substation to see how the local network is operating.

It's quite common for power to be restored briefly and go off again; this is our engineers using testing equipment to energise different sections of our network - this helps to narrow down any possible issues.

Our cabling network is often broken down by smaller sites, and our staff may also check these to test for any problems, and they may also walk the cabling route to see if there are any signs of malfunction that have appeared on the surface. Our time frame hasn't been adjusted, so for now I'll let you know more news as soon as our staff update us.

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.

Hello, Kevin from the messaging team here. Thanks for your patience today. 

Our records show that some properties affected by today's power cut may have had other unexpected interruptions recently, and although we don't believe today's issue to be related, we are sorry for further disruption. Power cuts are fully investigated by our engineers, but can sometimes happen multiple times before we can safely find the issue and fix it.

You may not have had other power cuts recently, but if you have, our customer services team will try to update as many customers as possible about our investigations. 

Our staff are still working to get your power back on as quickly as they can, and we'll update you again soon.

Sent by Kevin at 08:45 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is on our underground network that initially interrupted power to 537 properties.

However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 193 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment.

We're currently estimating power will be on between 10:00 and 11:00, but this may change depending on what our staff find.

Great news - Our engineers have now restored power to all 42 customers in the Turkey Cock Lane area by diverting power around an overhead network fault.

Thank you for your patience while our team worked hard to resolve this issue safely; we're pleased that our engineers were able restore the power within the timeframe we had estimated.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Sent by Lisa at 14:33 - Thanks for your patience today, we're happy to say our staff have now restored power to all but one property in the Barmouth Road area. I'm sorry for any disruption we caused by having to turn off your power to repair an unexpected underground network fault.

We'd still like to keep you informed if your electricity hasn't been restored. Please call us free any time on 08003163105 if we haven't already reached out to you by phone or in person at your property.

We apologise for the outage and understand that being without power can be challenging. Rest assured that our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep your lights on.

Hi there. We've just had a shift change, so Adam, Roland, Theo, Talie, Bev and Lisa are now here to keep you updated with progress from our field teams.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 10:51 - We can now confirm that all power in the Harley Street area should be back on.

Our staff are trying to find the root cause and fix it permanently, however with this kind of issue it's likely that power will go off multiple times before we're able to find and fix it. For now we have restored your power as quickly as we can.

If your power isn't on or you would like to talk to us, call us for free on 08003163105. We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
We're sorry your power has gone off again.

We believe there may be an underlying issue causing the recent power cuts, but due to the nature of the electricity network, the heat created by a developing fault can cause a break in a cable to reseal itself. This can make it difficult to locate, so while our engineers are testing our equipment thoroughly to pinpoint the issue, this kind of problem may interrupt power multiple times before it's possible for us to fix.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

I'll keep you updated. Sent by Adam at 10:05
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our staff have now arrived on site in the Harley Street area. They will start running tests at our substation to see how the local network is operating.

It's quite common for power to be restored briefly and go off again; this is our engineers using testing equipment to energise different sections of our network - this helps to narrow down any possible issues.

Our cabling network is often broken down by smaller sites, and our staff may also check these to test for any problems, and they may also walk the cabling route to see if there are any signs of malfunction that have appeared on the surface. Our time frame hasn't been adjusted, so for now I'll let you know more news as soon as our staff update us.

Sent by Adam at 09:41.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: A fault occurred on a piece of our electrical equipment which controls the power to your home.

Logs.Comment:
Sent by Adam at 10:51 - We can now confirm that all power in the Harley Street area should be back on.

Our staff are trying to find the root cause and fix it permanently, however with this kind of issue it's likely that power will go off multiple times before we're able to find and fix it. For now we have restored your power as quickly as we can.

If your power isn't on or you would like to talk to us, call us for free on 08003163105. We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

We're sorry your power has gone off again.

We believe there may be an underlying issue causing the recent power cuts, but due to the nature of the electricity network, the heat created by a developing fault can cause a break in a cable to reseal itself. This can make it difficult to locate, so while our engineers are testing our equipment thoroughly to pinpoint the issue, this kind of problem may interrupt power multiple times before it's possible for us to fix.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

I'll keep you updated. Sent by Adam at 10:05

Our staff have now arrived on site in the Harley Street area. They will start running tests at our substation to see how the local network is operating.

It's quite common for power to be restored briefly and go off again; this is our engineers using testing equipment to energise different sections of our network - this helps to narrow down any possible issues.

Our cabling network is often broken down by smaller sites, and our staff may also check these to test for any problems, and they may also walk the cabling route to see if there are any signs of malfunction that have appeared on the surface. Our time frame hasn't been adjusted, so for now I'll let you know more news as soon as our staff update us.

Sent by Adam at 09:41.

PlannedIncidentReason: We're carrying out planned work in your area. For our engineers to carry it out safely they need to turn the power off. We're doing this work as it's essential to provide reliable electricity supplies to your area. We're sorry for any inconvenience caused and thank you for your patience.
MainMessage: We have repaired the problem with the electricity supply in your area. There may still be some tidying up to do, and any details have been passed to our Maintenance department, who will attend to it as soon as possible. Please accept our sincere apologies for any inconvenience this may cause.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 14:19 - Our engineers have completed their investigations.

There's going to be a lot of work required to repair the issue safely, so we have decided to use temporary generators to provide power. To allow time for us to get the generators out to your area and connect them, we're now estimating we'll have power back on between 17:00 and 18:00.

We're sorry to change the time frame and will let you know when they arrive.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 09:54 - Some customers have told us that they want to be notified about a power cut before it happens. We're sorry we couldn't give you advance notice about this power cut. The issue happened suddenly and without warning, leaving us with no time to alert customers.
Our electricity network is regularly inspected and maintained to help prevent outages, but occasionally an unexpected fault can lead to an unplanned power cut. It's similar to a car that's been fully serviced and passed its MOT but still breaks down without warning.
Thank you for your understanding and we will keep you updated about todays outage until you are restored.

Steps.Message:
Hi there. We've just had a shift change, so Adam, Roland, Theo, Talie, Bev and Lisa are now here to keep you updated with progress from our field teams.

We know it's frustrating being without power, but we wanted to assure you we're still here and as soon as we have some more information we'll update you again.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 13:52 - Thanks for your patience over today.

Our engineers have let us know that excavations are continuing to expose more of our underground cabling for repairs. To give them time to do this safely, the estimated time frame to restore your power is now between 18:30 and 19:30.

We're sorry this is taking longer than expected, and we'll continue to keep you up to date, but if any change to this time comes in from our staff I'll let you know as soon as we hear.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Adam at 13:09 - Thanks for bearing with us over today, I just wanted to give you the latest from our engineers. Our staff have now confirmed that our substation equipment has operated due to an underground cable fault which we'll need to pinpoint, excavate, and repair. We have further specialist teams and equipment on site, and it's possible they will still be able to restore your power within the current time frame using other methods. However, once they've assessed further, we'll let you know if the time frame needs to change. For more information about this type of fault; here's a short video https://UKPN.short.gy/UG - I'll keep you updated.

Sent by Bev at 11:45 - We can now confirm that all power in the area should be back on. Our engineers had to unexpectedly turn off the power to safely repair a network fault in the Howard Drive area.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 12:18 - We can now confirm that all power in the Heddon Street area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected fault with some of our network equipment, which our engineers have now replaced.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 11:57 - Some customers have told us that they want to be notified about a power cut before it happens. We're sorry we couldn't give you advance notice about this power cut. The issue happened suddenly and without warning, leaving us with no time to alert customers.
Our electricity network is regularly inspected and maintained to help prevent outages, but occasionally an unexpected fault can lead to an unplanned power cut. It's similar to a car that's been fully serviced and passed its MOT but still breaks down without warning.
Thank you for your understanding and we will keep you updated about todays outage until you are restored.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Engineers have arrived on site to investigate what we believe to be an issue at the local substation in the Heddon Street area. Using an average timescale for this type of issue, we're looking to have you back on between 15:00 and 16:00, however we must stress that this time may change, depending on what our staff find. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can. We'll update you with all of our progress, but for more information about this type of fault; here's a short YouTube video https://UKPN.short.gy/fuse. Sent by Adam at 11:40.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Sent by Theo at 13:01 - Thanks for your patience today, we're happy to say our staff have now restored power to all but two properties in the Lewes and Uckfield area. I'm sorry for any disruption we caused by having to turn off your power due to an overhead cable on the ground.

We'd still like to keep you informed if your electricity hasn't been restored. Please call us free any time on 08003163105 if we haven't already reached out to you by phone or in person at your property.

We apologise for the outage and understand that being without power can be challenging. Rest assured that our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep your lights on.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Luke at 11:25 - Our engineers are on their way to investigate. We have been made aware that due to some overhead equipment coming down, our engineers have had to turn power off remotely in the interest of public safety.

We'll let you know once engineers get to site, and we'll turn your power back on once they have investigated and confirmed the area is safe.

We'll continue to keep you updated with our progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

We have been alerted to a potentially dangerous situation involving our overhead network, so have responded as quickly as possible and asked our control room to turn power off to the area using our high voltage network. This is why a wide area has lost electricity.

Once further specialist field staff have assessed the situation, we'll start diverting power back to affected homes as soon as they advise us it's safe. You may notice your lights going on and off as we work to get as much power restored as we can.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

We're sorry your power has now been off for several hours and we're working as quickly as possible to get you back on.

We know you may be concerned about your phone battery running low, so here are some tips on how you can prolong battery life: Set your phone to power saving mode, reduce screen brightness, turn off WiFi, Bluetooth and GPS (if not in use), cut apps running in the background and if possible top up using a car charger or portable battery pack.

Steps.Message: Sent by Adam at 13:44 - Thanks for bearing with us over today, I just wanted to give you the latest from our engineers. Our staff have now confirmed that our substation equipment has operated due to a third party damaging one of our underground cable which will require excavations and repairs. We're sending specialist teams and equipment to site, and it's possible they will still be able to restore your power within the current time frame using other methods. However, once they've assessed further, we'll let you know if the time frame needs to change. For more information about this type of fault; here's a short video https://UKPN.short.gy/UG - I'll keep you updated.

Sent by Bev at 14:30 - Our engineers have investigated and found that the power is off due to a fault on our underground electricity network which has affected our substation equipment, so we're sending a specialist team to pinpoint the fault and dig down to expose the cabling for repairs.

Considering the work that may be required, the revised estimated time to have the power back on is between 19:30 and 20:30.

We're sorry to change the time, but we'll keep you updated with our progress. If you have any questions or concerns, our team are always here to talk on 105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
I appreciate your continued patience this afternoon. I know having power cuts as often as this must be very disruptive, and our staff are doing everything they can to get this resolved.

They are still testing our network in the area, and depending on the local infrastructure this can take some time. However, once they update us we'll know if our estimated time frame needs to change and why, and I'll let you know as soon as we hear.

In the meantime, if we can offer any support or advice, our team are always available to talk on 105.

Sent by Kevin at 13:43.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our staff have now arrived on site in the Rose Lawn area. They will start running tests at our substation to see how the local network is operating.

It's quite common for power to be restored briefly and go off again; this is our engineers using testing equipment to energise different sections of our network - this helps to narrow down any possible issues.

Our cabling network is often broken down by smaller sites, and our staff may also check these to test for any problems, and they may also walk the cabling route to see if there are any signs of malfunction that have appeared on the surface. Our time frame hasn't been adjusted, so for now I'll let you know more news as soon as our staff update us.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 14:41 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Engineers have arrived on site to investigate what we believe to be an issue at the local substation in the Bentons Lane area. Using an average timescale for this type of issue, we're looking to have you back on between 19:30 and 20:30, however we must stress that this time may change, depending on what our staff find. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can. We'll update you with all of our progress, but for more information about this type of fault; here's a short YouTube video https://UKPN.short.gy/fuse. Sent by Roland at 14:29.
